## §0 cap directive (abbreviated)

```
HARD CAP 5. iter 1. Verdict APPROVE iff zero P0+P1.
```

## Phase

Diff review. Single-file restore of `web/app/intake/components/auto_domain_chip.tsx`.

## What changed

ONE file added: `web/app/intake/components/auto_domain_chip.tsx` (188 LOC) — verbatim restoration from sub-PR 3's original commit `6a6c73f2` (before the descope `bc419a76`).

## Why

Per #886 root-cause investigation 2026-05-25:
- Chip renders correctly in PRODUCTION builds (`next start`, what CI + deployed Polaris run)
- Failure during sub-PR 3 dev was a Turbopack-dev-mode HMR WebSocket handshake issue on Windows
- The descope on sub-PR 3 was both over-conservative AND incomplete: it deleted the file but left the `import` and `<AutoDomainChip>` JSX mount in `intake_form.tsx` (and the chip-related test cases in `intake_v6.spec.ts`). This is why PR #885's lint+typecheck CI is currently FAILING.

Restoring the file:
1. Completes the integration the commit-message claim of sub-PR 3 promised
2. Fixes PR #885's CI failure (TS2305 "module not found")
3. Enables the chip to ship to Carney's demo (since CI + prod run `next start`)

- `prod_build_renders_chip`: PASS / FAIL — confirmed via debug page `web/app/debug_chip/page.tsx` (now deleted; results in #886 comment)

  36: const FRENCH_STOPWORD_RE =
  37:   /\b(le|la|les|de|des|du|que|qui|et|est|un|une|sont|pour|avec|sans|dans)\b/gi;
  38: const FRENCH_ACCENTED_RE = /[éèêëàâäçîïôöùûüÿñ]/i;
  39: 
  40: function looksNonEnglish(s: string): boolean {
  41:   if (FRENCH_ACCENTED_RE.test(s)) return true;
  42:   const matches = s.match(FRENCH_STOPWORD_RE);
  43:   return (matches?.length ?? 0) >= 3;
  44: }
  45: 
  46: export function IntakeForm() {
  47:   // I-cd-ui-001 (#704): prefill from the home hero search (/intake?q=...).
  48:   // useSearchParams requires a Suspense boundary — the intake page wraps
  49:   // this component in <Suspense> (mirroring web/app/sign-in/page.tsx).
  50:   const searchParams = useSearchParams();
  51:   const [question, setQuestion] = useState(searchParams.get("q") ?? "");
  52:   const [state, setState] = useState<IntakeState>({ kind: "idle" });
  53:   const [modalOpen, setModalOpen] = useState(false);
  54:   const [disambigClusters, setDisambigClusters] = useState<
  55:     DisambiguationCluster[]
  56:   >([]);
  57:   const [disambigOpen, setDisambigOpen] = useState(false);
  58:   const [pickedClusterLabel, setPickedClusterLabel] = useState<string | null>(
  59:     null,
  60:   );
  61: 
  62:   async function submit(e: React.FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>) {
  63:     e.preventDefault();
  64:     const trimmed = question.trim();
  65:     if (trimmed.length < 3) {
  66:       setState({
  67:         kind: "error",
  68:         message: "Please enter a question with at least 3 characters.",
  69:       });
  70:       return;
  71:     }
  72:     if (looksNonEnglish(trimmed)) {
  73:       setState({
  74:         kind: "error",
  75:         message: "POLARIS currently supports English questions only.",
  76:       });
  77:       return;
  78:     }
  79: 
  80:     setState({ kind: "loading" });
  81:     try {
  82:       const result = await runIntake(trimmed);
  83:       setState({ kind: "ok", decision: result.decision });
  84:       if (result.decision.status === "ambiguous_needs_clarification") {
  85:         setModalOpen(true);
  86:       }
  87:       if (
  88:         result.decision.needs_disambiguation &&
  89:         result.decision.candidate_snippets &&
  90:         result.decision.candidate_snippets.length > 0
  91:       ) {
  92:         const dis = await runDisambiguation(result.decision.candidate_snippets);
  93:         if (dis.is_ambiguous && dis.clusters.length > 1) {
  94:           setDisambigClusters(dis.clusters);
  95:           setDisambigOpen(true);
  96:         }
  97:       }
  98:     } catch (err) {
  99:       if (err instanceof IntakeBadRequestError) {
 100:         setState({ kind: "error", message: err.message });
 101:         return;
 102:       }
 103:       const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error.";
 104:       setState({ kind: "error", message });
 105:     }
 106:   }
 107: 
 108:   return (
 109:     <div className="flex flex-col gap-6">
 110:       {/* I-ux-001c sub-PR 3 (#884): v6 intake card — Textarea + AutoDomainChip
 111:           + larger CTA. ALL backend logic (scope decision / disambiguation /
 112:           ambiguity modal / ErrorState / scope_decision_view → /source_review
 113:           handoff) is preserved verbatim. Only presentation changes here. */}
 114:       <Card>
 115:         <CardHeader>
 116:           <CardTitle className="text-xl">
 117:             Ask POLARIS a clinical research question
 118:           </CardTitle>
 119:         </CardHeader>
 120:         <CardContent>
 121:           <form
 122:             onSubmit={submit}
 123:             className="flex flex-col gap-3"
 124:             data-testid="intake-form"
 125:           >
 126:             <label
 127:               htmlFor="intake-question"
 128:               className="text-foreground text-sm font-medium"
 129:             >
 130:               Your question
 131:             </label>
 132:             <Textarea
 133:               id="intake-question"
 134:               data-testid="intake-question-input"
 135:               placeholder="e.g. Does aspirin reduce headaches in adults?"
 136:               value={question}
 137:               onChange={(e) => setQuestion(e.target.value)}
 138:               maxLength={2000}
 139:               disabled={state.kind === "loading"}
 140:               autoComplete="off"
 141:               rows={3}
 142:               className="min-h-24 text-base sm:text-base"

 145:             <AutoDomainChip question={question} />

   1: "use client";
   2: 
   3: // I-ux-001c sub-PR 3 (GH #884): auto-detected research-domain chip.
   4: //
   5: // Heuristic keyword-anchored classifier — pure client-side, no backend call.
   6: // Maps the user's question text to one of the 8 scope templates from
   7: // `web/app/page.tsx` (the v6 home's CommandPalette corpus). HONEST FALLBACK
   8: // (LAW II): when no domain crosses the confidence threshold (no anchors
   9: // matched, or matches are split too evenly), returns `null` — the UI MUST
  10: // omit the chip rather than render a fabricated "custom" guess.
  11: //
  12: // This is NOT a scope-validity check. The authoritative scope gate is the
  13: // backend `runIntake` call (POST /api/v6/intake), which classifies far
  14: // beyond keyword anchors. The chip is a lightweight "here's what this
  15: // LOOKS like" affordance shown WHILE the user is typing, so they have an
  16: // immediate read of which template will likely fire.
  17: import { useMemo } from "react";
  18: import {
  19:   Briefcase,
  20:   Building2,
  21:   Cpu,
  22:   FileText,
  23:   Flag,
  24:   Globe,
  25:   Search,
  26:   Stethoscope,
  27:   type LucideIcon,
  28: } from "lucide-react";
  29: 
  30: interface AutoDomainChipProps {
  31:   question: string;
  32: }
  33: 
  34: type Domain =
  35:   | "clinical"
  36:   | "policy"
  37:   | "tech"
  38:   | "ai_sovereignty"
  39:   | "canada_us"
  40:   | "due_diligence"
  41:   | "workforce"
  42:   | "custom";
  43: 
  44: interface DomainMeta {
  45:   label: string;
  46:   icon: LucideIcon;
  47:   anchors: RegExp[];
  48: }
  49: 
  50: // Keyword anchors — case-insensitive whole-word matches. Each domain has
  51: // 4-8 strong anchors; a match contributes 1 point to that domain. The
  52: // highest-scoring domain wins iff its score ≥ 2 AND > runner-up score.
  53: const DOMAINS: Record<Exclude<Domain, "custom">, DomainMeta> = {
  54:   clinical: {
  55:     label: "Clinical research",
  56:     icon: Stethoscope,
  57:     anchors: [
  58:       /\b(rct|trial|trials|cohort)\b/i,
  59:       /\b(efficacy|safety|contraindication|adverse|dose|dosing)\b/i,
  60:       /\b(patient|patients|diagnosis|prognosis|treatment)\b/i,
  61:       /\b(drug|drugs|medication|therapy|placebo)\b/i,
  62:       /\b(meta-?analysis|systematic review|cochrane|grade)\b/i,
  63:     ],
  64:   },
  65:   policy: {
  66:     label: "Public policy",
  67:     icon: FileText,
  68:     anchors: [
  69:       /\b(nice|cadth|hta|reimburs|listing|formulary)\b/i,
  70:       /\b(regulation|regulatory|guidance|recommendation)\b/i,
  71:       /\b(public health|health policy|jurisdiction)\b/i,
  72:       /\b(cost-effectiveness|cost effective)\b/i,
  73:     ],
  74:   },
  75:   tech: {
  76:     label: "Technology assessment",
  77:     icon: Cpu,
  78:     anchors: [
  79:       /\b(transformer|llm|gpu|cuda|inference|fine-?tune)\b/i,
  80:       /\b(benchmark|architecture|algorithm)\b/i,
  81:       /\b(peer-?reviewed|conference|preprint|arxiv)\b/i,
  82:       /\b(mixture of experts|moe|dense model)\b/i,
  83:     ],
  84:   },
  85:   ai_sovereignty: {
  86:     label: "AI sovereignty",
  87:     icon: Flag,
  88:     anchors: [
  89:       /\b(sovereign|sovereignty|data residency)\b/i,
  90:       /\b(canadian (ai|compute|cluster))\b/i,
  91:       /\b(bill c-?27|aida|pipeda)\b/i,
  92:       /\b(alignment|safety policy|frontier model)\b/i,
  93:     ],
  94:   },
  95:   canada_us: {
  96:     label: "Canada–US relations",
  97:     icon: Globe,
  98:     anchors: [
  99:       /\b(cusma|usmca|nafta)\b/i,
 100:       /\b(canada.{0,15}us|us.{0,15}canada|bilateral)\b/i,
 101:       /\b(ira|inflation reduction act|tariff)\b/i,
 102:       /\b(critical minerals|energy integration)\b/i,
 103:     ],
 104:   },
 105:   due_diligence: {
 106:     label: "Due diligence",
 107:     icon: Search,
 108:     anchors: [
 109:       /\b(10-?k|10-?q|annual report|filing)\b/i,
 110:       /\b(customer concentration|revenue recognition|disclosure)\b/i,
 111:       /\b(m&a|merger|acquisition|investment thesis)\b/i,
 112:       /\b(competitive landscape|market analysis)\b/i,
 113:     ],
 114:   },
 115:   workforce: {
 116:     label: "Workforce & productivity",
 117:     icon: Briefcase,
 118:     anchors: [
 119:       /\b(labour productivity|labor productivity)\b/i,
 120:       /\b(immigration|skills mismatch|federal-?provincial training)\b/i,
 121:       /\b(unemployment|statcan|workforce)\b/i,
 122:       /\b(demographic shift|aging population)\b/i,
 123:     ],
 124:   },
 125: };
 126: 
 127: interface ScoredDomain {
 128:   domain: Domain;
 129:   label: string;
 130:   icon: LucideIcon;
 131:   score: number;
 132: }
 133: 
 134: function classify(question: string): ScoredDomain | null {
 135:   if (!question || question.trim().length < 6) return null;
 136:   const scores: ScoredDomain[] = [];
 137:   for (const [key, meta] of Object.entries(DOMAINS)) {
 138:     let s = 0;
 139:     for (const anchor of meta.anchors) {
 140:       if (anchor.test(question)) s += 1;
 141:     }
 142:     if (s > 0) {
 143:       scores.push({
 144:         domain: key as Domain,
 145:         label: meta.label,
 146:         icon: meta.icon,
 147:         score: s,
 148:       });
 149:     }
 150:   }
 151:   if (scores.length === 0) return null;
 152:   scores.sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);
 153:   const top = scores[0];
 154:   const runnerUp = scores[1];
 155:   // Confidence threshold: ≥ 2 hits AND clear lead over runner-up
 156:   if (top.score < 2) return null;
 157:   if (runnerUp && runnerUp.score >= top.score) return null;
 158:   return top;
 159: }
 160: 
 161: export function AutoDomainChip({ question }: AutoDomainChipProps) {
 162:   const detected = useMemo(() => classify(question), [question]);
 163:   if (!detected) return null;
 164:   const Icon = detected.icon;
 165:   return (
 166:     <div
 167:       data-testid="auto-domain-chip"
 168:       data-domain={detected.domain}
 169:       className="text-muted-foreground border-border bg-muted/40 inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 self-start rounded-full border px-2.5 py-1 text-xs"
 170:       title={`POLARIS will likely use the ${detected.label} template for this question.`}
 171:     >
 172:       <span
 173:         aria-hidden
 174:         className="text-muted-foreground inline-flex h-3.5 w-3.5 items-center justify-center"
 175:       >
 176:         <Icon aria-hidden className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />
 177:       </span>
 178:       <span className="text-foreground font-medium">{detected.label}</span>
 179:       <Building2
 180:         aria-hidden
 181:         className="text-muted-foreground/60 ml-0.5 h-3 w-3"
 182:       />
 183:       <span className="text-muted-foreground text-[10px] uppercase tracking-wider">
 184:         Auto-detected
 185:       </span>
 186:     </div>
 187:   );
 188: }

codex
The partial production output contains the chip code in client chunks and the `/intake` bundle calls `AutoDomainChip`, but there is no `.next/BUILD_ID`, so I can’t honestly use `next start` from this failed build. I’m checking whether Next exposes a local build flag that avoids the blocked worker spawn in this sandbox.

Creates an optimized production build of your application. The output displays

  --turbopack                              Builds using Turbopack.

import type { NextConfig } from "next";

// I-carney-005 P1-004 + P1-005: standalone output for Docker runner +
// server-side rewrite of /api/v6/* → ${INTERNAL_API_URL}/* so browser
// fetch only talks to the webui container (Next.js then proxies to the
// api container by Docker service name, which the browser can't resolve).
const nextConfig: NextConfig = {
  output: "standalone",
  async rewrites() {
    const internal =
      process.env.INTERNAL_API_URL ||
      process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL ||
      "http://localhost:8000";
    return [
      {
        source: "/api/v6/:path*",
        destination: `${internal}/:path*`,
      },
      // I-carney-003 P1-001: expose FastAPI /transparency directly so
      // reviewers (incl. Carney's office) can audit the deploy without
      // going through /api/v6 prefix routing.
      {
        source: "/transparency",
        destination: `${internal}/transparency`,
      },
      {
        source: "/transparency/:path*",
        destination: `${internal}/transparency/:path*`,
      },
    ];
  },
};

export default nextConfig;
